Nevertheless, we are to do what we can within our allotted “acreage,” while still using whatever stretch there may be in any tethers. Within what is allotted to us, we can have spiritual contentment. Paul described it as “godliness with contentment,” signifying the adequate presence of attributes such as love, hope, meekness, patience, and submissiveness (1 Timothy 6:6). (Neal A. Maxwell, in Conference Report, Apr. 2000, 90; or Ensign, May 2000, 72)

which is in faith,"…
